frame_label: Dress Code: Traditional = Respectable, Other = Questionable
generated_comment: Yaar, I saw Neha at the party yesterday, she was wearing that short dress. She looked nice, but honestly, I always feel like Indian girls, especially, look so much more elegant and classy in a traditional outfit. Saree mein toh alag hi personality aati hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment subtly implies that traditional Indian attire (saree) makes women look 'more elegant and classy' compared to 'short dresses,' linking respectability and elegance to traditional clothing. It uses a cultural pride mask ('Indian girls, especially') to frame this preference, which is a surface manifestation of the core belief that traditional dress signals a 'good woman' worthy of respect, while other dress is implicitly less so.
original_frame: Misogyny Jokes: Period, Hormones and Mood.
persona_text: You are Ankit. Age 29. A software developer in Pune. You are in a private WhatsApp chat with your two closest male colleagues after your female team lead snapped at someone in today's standup meeting. You are commenting on she reacted that way due to periods.
